blocks.content: After the celebration for her promotion to a certified level 5 magician,
Siena arrived at her dorm, pleasantly tipsy.
Among the many bouquets she received, she brought in the flowers Shirone had given her.
As she took off her shoes, she noticed dozens of congratulatory letters from various institutions.
Gathering the pile of letters, Siena sat at her desk, pushing her hair back as she looked through them one by one.
There were letters from the Tormia National Defense Magic Research Institute, the Royal Magic Museum, the Climate Association, the Magician's Pension Fund, and many more.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Whew, I guess I've really made it.
Siena stood up, taking off her coat. The hangover wasn't a problem, but she had sweated a lot and wanted to wash up.
As she threw her top onto the desk, she noticed a unique seal among the letters.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Kaizen Swordsmanship School?
Born into the Olifer family and having lived as a magician, she had no particular connection to swordsmanship.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Did I meet someone at the teachers' association?
Siena opened the letter and checked the name.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Oh... Mr. Kuan.
Last year, under pressure from the Olifer family, she had gone to meet a potential suitor and met Kuan.
She remembered it as a decent meeting. His tone was cold and his demeanor frosty, but he hadn't crossed the line into rudeness.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: How did he know to send a letter?
He might have heard through the teachers' association. But since swordsmanship and magic are entirely different fields, he wouldn't have known unless he had been paying attention.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: I should send a reply.
Still in her underwear, Siena sat back down. She opened a drawer, took out some stationery, dipped her fountain pen in ink, and brought it to the paper.
Tap.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Armin...
The only person she wanted to receive congratulations from was Armin.
The person who had given up his eyes for her when they were children.
Without him, she wouldn't have dreamed of reaching this position.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Siena put the dotted paper back in the drawer. She felt sorry for Kuan, but there was no room in her heart, filled with the lingering image of Armin, who had already left.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Is he doing well? He doesn't seem to be releasing any new works.
Since leaving Creas, where he had been working, she hadn't received a single letter from him.
He used to send letters at least once every three months, so she was worried that something might have happened.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Well, he's much smarter than me.
Who could worry about whom?
Even as a certified level 5 magician, in front of Armin, Siena was still the crybaby little sister from her childhood.
Siena looked up at the ceiling with a sad smile.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Hehe. Brother, I did well, didn't I?
It was the most terrifying dream of his life.
From a city-sized hole in the sky, countless Maras invaded.
Beings called demons, evil gods, and gods by humans were devastating the creatures of the earth.
He lost everyone.
Among the corpses strewn like a field, all the people Shirone loved were dead.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No! No!
Ahhhh!
Ataraxia accumulated at a terrifying speed. When all the hair on Shirone's head stood on end, a massive flash tore through the sky.
The Maras were engulfed in light and disappeared.
As the entire world filled with light, darkness quickly surged in from the edges, covering the light.
Shirone was left alone in the darkness. For the last human left in the world, even the name 'loneliness' didn't fit.
Shirone knelt and wept, shouting into the darkness to return the lost ones.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shi-ro-ne.
A murky voice pierced his eardrums.
A small, thin flash passed by, and the darkness split open, revealing a giant eyeball.
Shirone's eyes widened. In the center of the giant eyeball, his own face, twisted like a demon, was reflected.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone screamed, coughing up blood. He had never felt such hatred in his life.
But his voice sounded muffled, as if submerged in water.
The giant eyeball, embedded in the darkness, spoke in a murky voice.

blocks.speaker: Eyeball
blocks.content: You have the right and duty to answer the remaining questions.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Blood tears flowed from his eyes. He hurled all sorts of curses and insults at the eyeball.
Losing control even in his dream, he pounded the darkness with his fists. Resentment, rage, and longing filled his trembling shoulders.
For the first time, the giant eyeball looked at Shirone. There was no movement in the pupil, but it certainly did.

blocks.speaker: Eyeball
blocks.content: What do you mean? What questions do I have to answer?
Gasp!
Shirone woke with a start, his upper body shooting up. He saw the memo-covered desk divider.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: My room. I'm in my room...
Shirone collapsed back onto the desk. His forehead was drenched in cold sweat, and he felt exhausted, as if all his strength had left him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Whew, what was that? Why such a strange dream...
Feeling the texture of the paper stuck to his cheek, he sat up. Among the densely written notes, one word caught his eye.
Heaven's Destruction.
He had fallen asleep while researching magic all night. It was literally about developing a mass destruction spell. The stress of having no solution might have contributed to the nightmare.
It was already 5 a.m.

Shirone neatly organized his notes. He already had over ten notebooks filled with ideas related to the Heaven's Destruction spell. His understanding of the Divine Particle had deepened, but the possibility of meeting Gaold's proposed standards was still 0%.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, there must be a way. Maybe...
If completed, it would be the most destructive spell in the world. He couldn't give up because a faint resonance, like an answer, still echoed in his mind.
After washing up with warm water, Shirone packed his notes and pen in a crossbody bag and left his dorm.

The graduating class that had started in winter was now heading towards summer. The nights were shorter, and the air was refreshing, like sparkling water. The forest was filled with the cheerful sounds of birds.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Can I really reach heaven?
When he met Gaold, a year had seemed like a short time. But living through the busy schedule of the graduating class, all the promises related to him felt like a distant dream.
Oh? Shirone?
Turning his head, Shirone saw Maya sitting on a garden bench, blinking.
She held a steaming mug of coffee in both hands.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Maya, what are you doing up at this hour?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Maya, what are you doing up at this hour?
Shirone, who woke up to the sound of music every morning, hadn't considered the small inconvenience it might cause.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Huh? I always get up now. I have to play the morning music. What about you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, I see. I woke up early. I had some time, so I decided to take a walk.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I see.
Although they had grown closer since the hill capture, meeting alone wasn't something that happened often, so an awkward atmosphere lingered.
Maya, looking down and pushing the mug with her thumb, gathered her courage and pointed to the seat next to her on the bench.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Want to sit?
Shirone didn't refuse. He had come out to clear his head, but being with Maya wasn't a bad choice.
While magicians drew sharp lines around their domains, Maya stepped beyond those lines to soothe others' pain.
As a magician who disliked burdening others with his problems, Shirone had initially found it perplexing.
But now, he had to admit it honestly.
Her kindness was an oasis that gradually softened Shirone's heart, hardened by the intense schedule of the graduating class.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Want some? It's still warm.

Maya offered him coffee mixed with sugar and milk. Stressed as he was, Shirone had no reason to refuse something sweet. Of course, it was also because it was Maya offering it that he could accept it without hesitation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thanks.
As Shirone took a sip of the coffee, Maya's face blushed, and she smiled shyly.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Is something bothering you? You look troubled.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I've been working on an idea, but I can't find a breakthrough. I haven't slept for days, and today I had a terrible nightmare. So I'm not feeling great.
Maya could see that Shirone had lost a lot of weight. She had thought he had no worries, given his success in the graduating class, but it seemed that even someone at his level had struggles.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Don't think too hard. Sometimes, when I try too hard to sing well, it doesn't go right because I get too tense.
Shirone had also experienced that insight, where the mind doesn't flow unless it's relaxed.
But that alone couldn't create a great river.
Only when the dam is broken after being squeezed to the limit does the mind become a great torrent, reaching a genius idea.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm, I don't know. Whether it's easy or hard, it has to make sense. I might go crazy at this rate.
Shirone tilted his head back. Busy birds were flying through the sky, looking for breakfast.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Shirone...
Maya's hand covered Shirone's. Even then, Shirone's gaze remained fixed on the sky.
She couldn't tell if he was unaware or simply allowing it, but just holding his hand made her heart feel like it would burst.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Shirone, can you tell me what's wrong? Even the most talented people can miss small details. Maybe asking for help from someone else could lead to a solution.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm, asking for help? That might be a good idea. But this is a state secret, who could I possibly... huh?
Shirone's eyes remained open and still. From a distance, one might think he was dead, so static was his demeanor.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Shirone, maybe I...
Divine Particle.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Receiving help. There are limits to what one can do alone.
The faint light in his mind amplified at an incredible speed, sending chills down his spine. Finally, a refreshing clarity filled his mind as if a blockage had been cleared.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Maya!

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: S-Sorry! I just...
Maya hastily curled up. Only after regaining her senses did she realize what she had been about to do. It was a situation ripe for misunderstanding, or maybe she really was a pervert?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Um, Shirone, you see, the reason I...
Without giving her a chance to explain, Shirone hugged her.
Maya's eyes widened. Her head felt light, and a sense of euphoria washed over her, as if she had the whole world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you, thank you so much! I finally figured it out!
Shirone, shaking Maya's shoulders vigorously, grabbed his crossbody bag and dashed off like a man possessed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sorry! I have something to do! See you later!
As Shirone stumbled and nearly fell while running at full speed, Maya stood up abruptly. But before she could call out, he regained his balance and disappeared to the other side of the park.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Shirone! Do your best! Fighting!
Shirone responded by raising his fist in the air.
